prompt_text:
Analyze the following java source code repository for violations of the Liskov Substitution Principle (LSP).

## Principle Definition
Subtypes must be substitutable for their base types without altering the correctness of the program. When a subclass overrides behavior in a way that breaks the base class contract, it violates LSP.

## What to Look For
- Subclasses that override methods and change their expected behavior
- Subclasses that raise exceptions not raised by the parent
- Subclasses tha...
raw_response:
{
  "findings": [
    {
      "file_path": "src/main/java/tools/jackson/core/json/UTF8DataInputJsonParser.java",
      "entity_name": "UTF8DataInputJsonParser.nextToken",
      "entity_type": "method",
      "line_start": 361,
      "line_end": 375,
      "principle": "LSP",
      "severity": "high",
      "description": "Method violates the contract of JsonParser.nextToken() by throwing IOException instead of only JacksonException.",
      "reasoning": "The base class JsonParser defines nextToken() to throw JacksonException (a runtime exception). The implementation in UTF8DataInputJsonParser wraps this in a try-catch block for IOException and throws it, which forces callers to handle checked exceptions not specified in the base class contract, breaking substitution."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "src/main/java/tools/jackson/core/json/UTF8DataInputJsonParser.java",
      "entity_name": "UTF8DataInputJsonParser.nextName",
      "entity_type": "method",
      "line_start": 579,
      "line_end": 585,
      "principle": "LSP",
      "severity": "high",
      "description": "Method violates the contract of JsonParser.nextName() by throwing IOException instead of only JacksonException.",
      "reasoning": "Similar to nextToken(), nextName() is defined in the base class to throw only JacksonException. By wrapping the logic in a try-catch for IOException and rethrowing, the subclass forces callers to catch checked exceptions, which is incompatible with the expected behavior of the base class."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "src/main/java/tools/jackson/core/json/ReaderBasedJsonParser.java",
      "entity_name": "ReaderBasedJsonParser._decodeEscaped",
      "entity_type": "method",
      "line_start": 1056,
      "line_end": 1062,
      "principle": "LSP",
      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "Method signature in subclass forces checked exception handling not present in base class.",
      "reasoning": "While the method is protected, the pattern of catching IOException and wrapping it into a JacksonException is inconsistent across parser implementations. In this specific case, the implementation relies on the base class behavior but forces a wrapper that alters the signature expectations of internal components."
    }
  ]
}

findings.description: Method violates the contract of JsonParser.nextToken() by throwing IOException instead of only JacksonException.
findings.reasoning: The base class JsonParser defines nextToken() to throw JacksonException (a runtime exception). The implementation in UTF8DataInputJsonParser wraps this in a try-catch block for IOException and throws it, which forces callers to handle checked exceptions not specified in the base class contract, breaking substitution.

findings.description: Method violates the contract of JsonParser.nextName() by throwing IOException instead of only JacksonException.
findings.reasoning: Similar to nextToken(), nextName() is defined in the base class to throw only JacksonException. By wrapping the logic in a try-catch for IOException and rethrowing, the subclass forces callers to catch checked exceptions, which is incompatible with the expected behavior of the base class.

findings.description: Method signature in subclass forces checked exception handling not present in base class.
findings.reasoning: While the method is protected, the pattern of catching IOException and wrapping it into a JacksonException is inconsistent across parser implementations. In this specific case, the implementation relies on the base class behavior but forces a wrapper that alters the signature expectations of internal components.
